refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (local vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational data.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5 days). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage), elapsed time, safety incidents, and policy violations. Calculate productivity_achieved = total_volume / total_time. Verify productivity_achieved ≥ productivity_target and zero safety plan violations.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (local or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to sustain target cycle time.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The telemetry and tasking systems shall log task execution data (volume, time, location) for productivity measurement.
FR-NGHE-001.4: Safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies without blocking legitimate task execution.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-precision hydraulic actuation, real-time GNSS/IMU positioning, and operator feedback loops to minimize cycle time variance and maximize payload per cycle.

DP-B: Implement dual-mode operator interface (local cab + remote teleoperation) with low-latency video/sensor fusion and haptic feedback to maintain situational awareness and control fidelity across both modes.
DP-C: Deploy continuous telemetry pipeline (vehicle → cloud/edge) capturing task events (dig start/end, load mass, position, time) and expose via Telemetry interface for productivity analytics.

DP-D: Embed safety policy enforcement layer (geofence, speed limits, stability checks, emergency stop) that monitors task execution in real-time and raises SafetyAlert when policy violation is imminent or detected.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-Integrated: Combine DP-A (vehicle precision), DP-B (dual-mode operator interface), DP-C (telemetry pipeline), and DP-D (safety enforcement) as a coordinated design solution. Productivity emerges from the synergy of precise vehicle control, responsive operator interface, continuous task logging, and safety guardrails that prevent costly incidents.

potential_sources: Vehicle actuation precision (DP-A) depends on operator control fidelity (DP-B); low latency in teleoperation is critical.
Telemetry pipeline (DP-C) must capture events triggered by vehicle control (DP-A) and operator commands (DP-B).
Safety enforcement (DP-D) must not introduce latency that degrades operator responsiveness (DP-B) or cycle time (DP-A).
All DPs depend on reliable Network Link interface for remote teleoperation and telemetry.
coupling_check.notes: Couplings are manageable via interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). No circular dependencies detected. Recommend explicit latency budgets and event ordering guarantees in interface specifications.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Operator training and worksite conditions are representative of target deployment; productivity_target is achievable with proposed design.
Risk: Teleoperation latency (network jitter, video codec delay) may degrade operator responsiveness below acceptable threshold, reducing cycle time.
Risk: Safety enforcement (geofence, stability checks) may introduce false positives that block legitimate task execution, reducing effective productivity.
Risk: Telemetry data loss or clock skew may corrupt productivity measurement, invalidating trial results.
Risk: High safety criticality may require redundant safety channels, increasing cost and complexity.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B and DP-D with explicit latency and false-positive budgets. Define 'representative operations' in terms of soil type, slope, payload range, and operator skill level. Establish telemetry data integrity requirements (e.g., CRC, timestamp validation).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration boundary encompassing vehicle, teleoperation, safety services, and telemetry pipeline. Owns end-to-end productivity delivery and safety compliance. Interfaces with external worksite systems (e.g., fleet management, safety audit logs).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits task execution events (dig start/end, load mass, position, timestamp, cycle time) to external analytics or fleet management system for productivity measurement and reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task assignments (e.g., 'excavate zone A, load into truck B') from external dispatcher or site manager, translates into vehicle control commands and operator guidance.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits safety alerts (policy violation, geofence breach, stability warning, emergency stop) to external safety management system and on-site safety personnel.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses network link for remote teleoperation (video, control commands, sensor data) and telemetry uplink. Critical for low-latency remote operation.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary. All sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are logically contained. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross boundary. This is the correct assignment for a system-level FR.

Vehicle module owns actuation precision (DP-A) and contributes to telemetry (DP-C). However, operator interface (DP-B) and safety enforcement (DP-D) are not primarily vehicle responsibilities. Assigning FR to Vehicle alone would diffuse productivity ownership across multiple modules and obscure system-level integration.

Teleoperation module owns operator interface (DP-B) but does not own vehicle control (DP-A), telemetry (DP-C), or safety enforcement (DP-D). Assigning FR to Teleoperation alone would fragment productivity responsibility and create coupling across module boundaries.

Safety Services owns safety enforcement (DP-D) but does not own vehicle control, operator interface, or telemetry. Assigning FR to Safety Services would misalign ownership; safety is an enabler of productivity, not the primary driver.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Productivity is a system-level emergent property arising from the coordinated interaction of vehicle precision, operator interface, telemetry, and safety enforcement. No single sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) can own this FR in isolation. NGHE Solution is the correct boundary because it integrates all four DPs and owns the end-to-end task execution pipeline.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution is a high-level integration boundary. Detailed design and implementation will require decomposition into Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services sub-modules. Ensure interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are clearly defined to avoid diffusion during detailed design.

Risk: Productivity measurement depends on accurate telemetry. If telemetry is owned by a sub-module (e.g., Vehicle) and not properly integrated into NGHE Solution, measurement may be incomplete or unreliable.

Risk: Safety enforcement (Safety Services) may conflict with productivity optimization (Vehicle, Teleoperation). Ensure safety policies are validated against productivity targets during design review.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that NGHE Solution owns the integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. Define explicit interface contracts for Telemetry (task event schema, timestamp format, data integrity), Tasking (task format, priority, cancellation), SafetyAlert (alert types, severity levels, response actions), and Network Link (latency budget, bandwidth, redundancy). Establish a system-level productivity measurement plan that specifies data sources, aggregation logic, and validation criteria.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR (operator executes tasks with measurable productivity ≥ target while maintaining safety compliance) is directly addressed by the selected DP (integrated vehicle precision, dual-mode operator interface, telemetry pipeline, and safety enforcement). The DP is logically sound and feasible. No circular dependencies or contradictions detected.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry pipeline (DP-C) spans Vehicle (sensor data source) and NGHE Solution (integration and external interface). Ensure clear ownership: Vehicle emits raw sensor data, NGHE Solution aggregates and translates into task events.

Safety enforcement (DP-D) may be implemented in Safety Services module but must be coordinated with Vehicle control (DP-A) and Teleoperation (DP-B). Define safety policy enforcement points (e.g., pre-command validation, post-command monitoring).
Operator interface (DP-B) spans Teleoperation module (remote) and Vehicle module (local cab). Ensure consistent control semantics and latency characteristics across both modes.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary for this FR because it owns the integration of all four DPs. Sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) will implement specific DPs but must expose interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) to NGHE Solution for system-level coordination. No diffusion of ownership; clear module boundaries.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via representative operations trial. Productivity can be measured from telemetry data (task events, timestamps, volumes). Safety compliance can be verified from SafetyAlert logs and worksite safety audit records. Recommend establishing baseline productivity_target and trial protocol before detailed design.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR is allocated to NGHE Solution with clear sub-FRs for Vehicle (task execution), Teleoperation (operator interface), Telemetry (data logging), and Safety Services (policy enforcement). Each sub-FR can be traced to a specific DP and sub-module. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Establish explicit productivity_target value (e.g., 150 m³/h) and representative operations protocol (soil type, slope, payload, operator skill, trial duration) before proceeding to detailed design.
Define telemetry data schema (task event types, fields, timestamp format, data integrity checks) to ensure accurate productivity measurement.
Establish latency budget for teleoperation (e.g., <200 ms round-trip) to ensure operator responsiveness is not degraded.
Define safety policy enforcement rules (geofence, speed limits, stability checks) and validate against productivity targets to ensure safety does not unnecessarily block task execution.
Establish Network Link reliability requirements (uptime, bandwidth, redundancy) to support both remote teleoperation and telemetry uplink.

decision.justification: The refined FR is well-formed, the selected DP is logically sound and addresses the FR, and the assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is the correct system-level boundary. All gates pass. However, the FR is not atomic; it contains four distinct sub-FRs (vehicle task execution, operator interface, telemetry, safety enforcement) that require further decomposition. Proceeding to the next level will clarify ownership, interface contracts, and implementation details for each sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). This is a normal and healthy progression in functional decomposition.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (local or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to sustain target cycle time.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The telemetry and tasking systems shall log task execution data (volume, time, location) for productivity measurement.
FR-NGHE-001.4: Safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies without blocking legitimate task execution.

assumptions: Productivity_target is achievable with proposed design (vehicle precision, operator interface, telemetry, safety enforcement).
Operator training and worksite conditions are representative of target deployment.
Network Link provides sufficient latency and bandwidth for remote teleoperation and telemetry uplink.
Safety policies can be formalized as enforceable rules (geofence, speed limits, stability checks) without excessive false positives.
Telemetry data is accurate and complete for productivity measurement.
open_risks: Teleoperation latency may degrade operator responsiveness and reduce cycle time below target.
Safety enforcement may introduce false positives that block legitimate task execution.
Telemetry data loss or clock skew may corrupt productivity measurement.
High safety criticality may require redundant safety channels, increasing cost and complexity.
Conflict between safety policies and productivity optimization may require trade-off analysis.
evidence_needed: Baseline productivity_target value and representative operations protocol (soil type, slope, payload, operator skill, trial duration).
Telemetry data schema and integrity requirements.
Teleoperation latency budget and operator responsiveness acceptance criteria.
Safety policy rules (geofence, speed limits, stability checks) and validation against productivity targets.
Network Link reliability requirements (uptime, bandwidth, redundancy).
Representative operations trial results demonstrating productivity ≥ target and zero safety plan violations.
